ogDescription: Emergency veterinary care for pet families across South Florida. Find your city and learn how to reach our Fort Lauderdale hospital.
main:
South Florida

Emergency Vet Care for South Florida Pet Families.

Pet Emergency Center is a single hospital in Fort Lauderdale that serves families across Broward, Palm Beach, and Miami-Dade counties. When the closest 24-hour clinic is not the right one, this is where our neighbors come.

Call (954) 772-0420 Get Directions
One hospital, regional reach Open weeknights and 24/7 on weekends Independent and locally owned
Fort Lauderdale
Why families travel to us

Why South Florida Families Come to Pet Emergency Center.

South Florida has more than one option for emergency veterinary care, and we know that. Some are nationally branded specialty hospitals with high price tags. Some are general clinics that handle emergencies on the side. Pet Emergency Center is something different: a single, independently owned hospital in Fort Lauderdale, focused only on emergency and critical care, run by veterinarians who live in this community.

That model is why families across Broward, southern Palm Beach, and northern Miami-Dade make the drive to us. Care is handled by the same small team night after night. Pricing is explained before treatment begins. Decisions are not made by a corporate boardroom three states away.

Read our story →

Where our families come from

Cities and Communities We Serve.

Pick your city for directions, what we treat, and how the visit works. If your community is not listed, you are still welcome. Our hospital is open to families across South Florida.

Fort Lauderdale

Our hospital is located in Fort Lauderdale, just off Cypress Creek Road. We are the after-hours practice many local daytime vets refer to.

Emergency vet in Fort Lauderdale →

Pompano Beach

Just north of our hospital. Quick access by Federal Highway, US-1, or I-95. Many of our regulars live here.

Emergency vet in Pompano Beach →

Coral Springs

Northwest of us. Direct route by Atlantic Boulevard or the Sawgrass Expressway from many neighborhoods.

Emergency vet in Coral Springs →

Boca Raton

North into southern Palm Beach County. We are a common after-hours option when local Boca clinics close.

Emergency vet in Boca Raton →

Hollywood, FL

South in Broward, near the Hollywood Beach communities. Reachable by I-95, US-1, or Federal Highway.

Emergency vet in Hollywood →

Weston

West in Broward, off I-75 and I-595. A short drive across the county for families in Weston Hills and Bonaventure.

Emergency vet in Weston →

Davie

West Broward, large lots and equestrian properties. A short drive across I-595 and I-95.

Emergency vet in Davie →

Where we are

One Hospital, Centrally Located.

Pet Emergency Center is at 921 East Cypress Creek Road, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33334. The hospital sits just off I-95 between commercial Boulevard and Cypress Creek Road, which makes it reachable from most of central and southeastern Broward in a short drive. Families coming from northern Broward and Palm Beach typically use I-95 south. Families coming from western Broward use I-595 east.

If you are unsure of the best route from where you are right now, open Google Maps. The map at the bottom of this page also has driving directions one tap away.

A few practical notes

A Few Things Worth Knowing About the Drive.

If you are calling us from outside Broward, we will help you decide whether driving in is right for the situation or whether your pet is better served at a closer hospital. Honest triage is part of how we practice. We will not ask you to drive an hour if a clinic five minutes from you can handle the case.

If you do drive to us, plan for parking on site, free of charge. Walk-ins are welcome at any time during our open hours.

If your pet is in active distress, call us first. We can advise on whether to drive or call a closer hospital before you leave.

Compassionate care. Always here.

Wherever You Are in South Florida, We Are Ready.

Call us, walk in during open hours, or book a telehealth consultation if you would rather talk to a veterinarian from home first. Whichever you choose, the same team of experienced emergency vets will help you decide what is best for your pet.

Call (954) 772-0420 Book a Telehealth Consultation
